questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: responsible / are / for / who / safety / miners / ensuring
options: Responsible for miners safety who is
Miners safety who is responsible for ensuring
Who is responsible for ensuring miners safety
Ensuring safety for miners who is responsible
questions.solution: Who is responsible for ensuring miners safety

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: She is (A) / more taller (B) / than her (C) / younger sister. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: monitoring / technology / improved / has / safety / mine
options: Improved technology has mine safety monitoring
Monitoring mine safety has technology improved
Technology has improved mine safety monitoring
Mine safety monitoring technology has improved
questions.solution: Technology has improved mine safety monitoring

questions.stem: Rearrange: is / of / coal / formation / a / process / slow
options: Coal formation is a slow process
A slow process is formation of coal
Formation of coal is a slow process
Coal is a slow process of formation
questions.solution: Coal formation is a slow process

Mining industry is a important sector in India
Mining is an important sector in India
In India mining is important sector
questions.solution: Mining is an important sector in India

options: Successfully the team completed the project
The team successfully completed the project
The project successfully completed the team
Completed the project the team successfully
questions.solution: The team successfully completed the project

options: Innovation is key for economic growth

questions.solution: Innovation is key for economic growth

questions.solution: Safety should be our top priority in mines

Announced a new mining policy the government has
questions.solution: The government has announced a new mining policy

options: He asked that I convey him my regards.
He asked me to convey to him my regards.
He asked me to convey my regards to him.
He asked me to convey him my regards.
questions.solution: He asked me to convey to him my regards.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A balanced 3-phase load draws 20 A at 415 V line, pf 0.85. The total power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flicker:
options: Voltage fluctuations (0.5-30 Hz) causing lamp flicker
questions.solution: Flicker from arc furnaces, welders, motor starting. Pst < 1 per IEC 61000.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Introducing a small air gap into an iron-cored magnetic circuit, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: The flux decreases for the same MMF.
The inductance of a coil on it decreases.
questions.solution: An air gap adds large reluctance (low permeability), so total reluctance rises and flux/inductance fall.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The form factor of a sinusoidal waveform is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Increasing the air gap in a magnetic circuit (other things equal):
questions.solution: The air gap adds large reluctance (low permeability), so for the same MMF the flux falls.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a 100 µF capacitor charged to 100 V is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CRO measures:
options: Voltage, time period, frequency, phase difference
questions.solution: CRO: electron beam deflected by voltage. Lissajous figures for phase and frequency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Battery charging: CC-CV
questions.solution: Lead-acid: CC at C/10 then CV at 2.3-2.4V/cell. Float: 2.25V/cell.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A sinusoidal voltage has a peak value of 325 V. Its RMS value is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An induction motor has air-gap power 30 kW and slip 0.03. The rotor copper loss is:
questions.solution: Rotor copper loss = s × Pag = 0.03 × 30 kW = 900 W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: R1=22Ω, R2=10Ω in parallel. Rp:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method the readings are W₁ = 1000 W and W₂ = 200 W. The total 3-phase power is:
questions.solution: Total power = W₁ + W₂ = 1000 + 200 = 1200 W (the difference relates to reactive power).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a 1000 µF capacitor charged to 20 V is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Armature reaction in alternator:
options: Distorts, opposes or aids main field depending on PF
questions.solution: UPF: cross-magnetizing. Lagging: demagnetizing. Leading: magnetizing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Alternator frequency: f = P·N/(120)
questions.solution: N = speed in rpm. P = number of poles. f = P·N/120.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Synchronous motor starting:
questions.solution: Damper winding: induction motor action brings rotor near synchronous speed. Then DC field applied.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: R1=220Ω, R2=220Ω in series, V=24V. Current:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As the slip of an induction motor increases beyond the value at maximum torque, the developed torque:
questions.solution: Torque rises with slip up to the breakdown point, then falls as slip increases further (the unstable region).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Power in 33Ω at 20V:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 10 Ω, Q = 200 Ω and S = 40 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 10×40/200 = 2 Ω.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a balanced delta connection, the line current is related to the phase current by:
questions.solution: In delta, line and phase voltages are equal and IL = √3·Iph.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PMMC (permanent-magnet moving-coil) instrument can directly measure:
questions.solution: PMMC responds to average value; on AC the average is zero, so it reads DC directly (needs a rectifier for AC).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer with 200 primary and 1000 secondary turns is fed 400 V. The secondary voltage is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An auto-transformer is more economical than a two-winding transformer mainly because:
options: Part of the power is transferred conductively, saving copper
questions.solution: In an auto-transformer only part of the power is transformed inductively; the conductive path reduces copper and size, best for ratios near 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Wheatstone bridge:
questions.solution: For medium R (1Ω-1MΩ). Balance: zero galvanometer current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DC motor speed: N ∝ (V - I_a·R_a)/φ
questions.solution: Shunt: φ constant → nearly constant speed. Series: φ∝I_a → variable speed.
